title: Machine Learning 及 Artifical Intelligence 蛀書區

replies.msg:
小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/
lm
留名

漏咗本
Semi-Supervised Learning. Olivier Chapelle, Bernhard Schölkopf, and Alexander Zien
http://www.acad.bg/ebook/ml/MITPress-%20SemiSupervised%20Learning.pdf
上述除咗 information retrieval 都屬於基本 ML 書，冇太深既數學同 stat，內容都唔係太專向某類 application

quote.msg: 小弟係engine 撚， ug 個時讀過幾個maths course, 包括calculus, linear algebra 同prob and stat. 如果想了解ML 你會建議由邊本書起手？

replies.msg:
入門嘅ML主要都係要識 Gaussian distribution/conditional probability/Bayes theorem 同埋 differentiation/integration/少少partial differentiation (簡單嗰啲夠皮，唔使integration by part)。不過最緊要最緊要最緊要都係matrix algebra, 啲eigenvalue 同 eigenvector 成日周不時都會走嚟搵你㗎。Programming 嘅話就一定係Python，因爲Python有好多library畀你玩。

個人覺得冇話邊本書對初學者嚟講係最好，因爲我都係一路睇網上面啲blog/tutorial (medium.com 好多都有用圖嚟解釋啲algorithm，比起textbook冇咁悶) 同埋example code玩玩下學返嚟。learn by doing始終都係會有大啲成功感，起碼唔會俾啲proof of correctness同啲algorithm嘅implementation detail嚇親搞到冇嗮癮先。
ML有個好處就在於你可以將啲ML algorithm攞嚟plug + play，當lego嚟砌，所以有時就真係未必要食嗮全部theory先可以砌model，當然做研究另計啦。

btw colab.research.google.com 是個好東西，可以喺browser度寫Python code同有限度噉免費使用GPU。唔得嘅話就可以用Jupyter notebook，依啲嘢好多時data science係都會用嘅

quote.msg: 小弟係engine 撚， ug 個時讀過幾個maths course, 包括calculus, linear algebra 同prob and stat. 如果想了解ML 你會建議由邊本書起手？

replies.msg: 可以直接睇 deep learning 同 introduction to statistical learning with R。都可以補一補 numerical optimization 既底，cambridge press 有本書叫: Convex optimization 但非免費。Online course 就跟 Andrew Ng

追加:
Optimization theory:

Constrained Optimization and Lagrange Multiplier Methods. Dimitri P. Bertsekas.
http://web.mit.edu/dimitrib/www/Convex_Alg_Chapters.html

Comment: 唔識 Lagrange multiplier, KKT condition, Primal/Dual problem 既話基本上你可以放棄 regularization 及 support vector machine 既 theory

Scientific Computing: An Introductory Survey. Michael Heath.
http://www-e6.ijs.si/~roman/files/tmp/M.Heath-SComputing/scientific-computing-michael-t-heath.pdf

Comment: 可以睇到 Optimization 嗰 part，ODE/PDE 比較 physics/finance oriented ，唔太關入門 deep learning 事，但 ODE/PDE 本身同 neural network 係 advanced research 上係有關係的

Genetic algorithm 係值得一睇既 topic ，但比較好既書都冇免費版

Linear algebra, functional analysis, multivariable calculus, basic analysis, probability theory 好多 ref 都可以睇，Online course 同 youtube video 都唔少，因為比較 general 我就唔特別去討論

NUMERICAL LINEAR ALGEBRA. Lloyd N. Trefethen and David Bau
https://www.ime.unicamp.br/~pulino/MS512/livros/trefethen/

Comment: Unsupervised learning 建議都睇下 numerical lin alg，如果 focus 係 supervised learning 可以暫時跳過

Bayesian Statistics:
Bayesian Data Analysis, by Andrew Gelman, John Carlin, Hal Stern, David Dunson, Aki Vehtari, and Donald Rubin.
http://www.stat.columbia.edu/~gelman/book/

Q: 係咪要睇曬所有書?
A: 唔係, 但應該睇曬/以前 course covered 過，如果發現有陌生 topic 就不妨開黎睇下，大學上堂其實好有限

Q: 數學好深喎，咁都夠膽講基礎?
A: Entschuldigung, 數學程度因人而異，建議慢慢黎 我只係敢講係 elementary + introductory，唔保證係 easy

Q: 但我出黎做嘢都唔洗咁多數學...
A: 的確係，正如你唔會信一個唔識 calculus 既 engineer 或者精算師，我都唔會信一位唔識 calculus 同 optimization 既 data scientist

Library 確實要 call，call 邊隻 ，call 咗結果點處理都係超越咗 programming 問題。盲 call 不如 automl/買 service，可能結果更稱心

quote.msg: 小弟係engine 撚， ug 個時讀過幾個maths course, 包括calculus, linear algebra 同prob and stat. 如果想了解ML 你會建議由邊本書起手？

入門嘅ML主要都係要識 Gaussian distribution/conditional probability/Bayes theorem 同埋 differentiation/integration/少少partial differentiation (簡單嗰啲夠皮，唔使integration by part)。不過最緊要最緊要最緊要都係matrix algebra, 啲eigenvalue 同 eigenvector 成日周不時都會走嚟搵你㗎。Programming 嘅話就一定係Python，因爲Python有好多library畀你玩。

個人覺得冇話邊本書對初學者嚟講係最好，因爲我都係一路睇網上面啲blog/tutorial (medium.com 好多都有用圖嚟解釋啲algorithm，比起textbook冇咁悶) 同埋example code玩玩下學返嚟。learn by doing始終都係會有大啲成功感，起碼唔會俾啲proof of correctness同啲algorithm嘅implementation detail嚇親搞到冇嗮癮先。
ML有個好處就在於你可以將啲ML algorithm攞嚟plug + play，當lego嚟砌，所以有時就真係未必要食嗮全部theory先可以砌model，當然做研究另計啦。

btw colab.research.google.com 是個好東西，可以喺browser度寫Python code同有限度噉免費使用GPU。唔得嘅話就可以用Jupyter notebook，依啲嘢好多時data science係都會用嘅

呢d唔算係咩理論啦 求其一個ML course都會講到啦 講緊你實際上點操作啊 你實戰較learning rate係咪又係求其較高d 低d去試啊 會唔會theoretically derive loss topology同learning rate之間既關係啊？如果唔會既話，其實無必要睇咁多書㗎其實，直接開始玩model玩下parameter，train唔到有咩bottleneck咪睇番書囉 拎咁多本書出黎嚇layman咩

replies.msg:
另外澄清，我呢個 post 啲書並唔係比新手 starting point，你自己追 post 呢個 post 我推薦比 layman 都係 coursera 同 basic stat course

你呢種輕視基礎知識嘅態度完全同 Ai / ds bootcamp 班人不謀而合，睇到都令人火滾，串串貢以為自己識做 parameter selection 就等同所有操作，老實講 automl 可能會好過你。依你嘅邏輯，不如 software developer 唔好寫 documentation ，咁長都係寫黎嚇人嘅姐 實際操作都係上 stackoverflow copy code

有少少離題，但我反而好奇呢類工係香港嘅情況係點，香港應該重application遠多於theory，而且真係知道自己做緊咩嘅公司好似唔多，同埋好似冇個master都好難爭，要用最短嘅時間employable應該點行

同埋呢行都比較新，前景都有D懷疑，而且比起IT嗰邊math、stat等都有較高要求，但待遇又差幾遠
Lm
唉想當年CV都唔係用NN嘅

quote.msg:
小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/

quote.msg:
夠同唔夠好因人而異，如果主要做 BI/Database 呢類 data analyst ，對 database/software 認知重要啲。如果係 software engineer 嘅話，call library 係夠，因為最後都唔係自己去搞

In case 係 data scientist / ML engineer 或者 tech role ，call library 之餘你都要清楚自己做咩

好簡單舉個例子，過去嘅一個工作例子，clustering ，Call hierarchical, k means 或者 DBSCAN 好多人都識。但係現實嘅問題係，call 完之後個 results 係咪 reliable? 跟著落黎嘅 follow up task 都可能取決於個 results 我相信呢啲細節位取決於 domain knowledge + technical knowledge ，而且非 programming 問題。特別 unsupervised learning 情況

Library 確實要 call，call 邊隻 ，call 咗結果點處理都係超越咗 programming 問題。盲 call 不如 automl/買 service，可能結果更稱心

domain knowledge
呢樣嘢真係要強調，data science其中一環重點
prior distribution, cluster number, feature engineering 呢啲都係 domain knowledge 入面拎返出黎

replies.msg: 我對呢個範疇研究冇太多，依家 ai 好多時都係啲 function approximation 嘅嘢，叫做 learning 係因為有入有出。而且一個 ai，無論形式存在，有冇自我意識，我覺得都係睇佢有冇能力去 train 一個 function given 個 model 係模仿到生物學嘅大腦。咁當中嘅問題就係可唔可以收窄一下 model 同人腦嘅差異。但人對自己個腦認知都唔太多，當中又有道德問題可能令到某類研究做唔到 有另一種講法係，ai 唔一定需要有模仿人腦，只要可以持續接受到外界資訊，然後做到邏輯推論就可以。但依家 ai 只可以係指定 input 做到 classification 呢類簡類工作，未算係邏輯推論 。仲有好多睇法，因為大家對 consciousness 冇乜一致定義
btw neural network 同人腦構造冇直接科學上關係，佢只係一堆 functions ，透過改變唔同參數達致 output 同 prediction 嘅差異變細。真正關事應該係 neural computing 不過我都係冇乜研究

Q: 係咪要睇曬所有書?
A: 唔係, 但應該睇曬/以前 course covered 過，如果發現有陌生 topic 就不妨開黎睇下，大學上堂其實好有限

Q: 數學好深喎，咁都夠膽講基礎?
A: Entschuldigung, 數學程度因人而異，建議慢慢黎 我只係敢講係 elementary + introductory，唔保證係 easy

Q: 但我出黎做嘢都唔洗咁多數學...
A: 的確係，正如你唔會信一個唔識 calculus 既 engineer 或者精算師，我都唔會信一位唔識 calculus 同 optimization 既 data scientist

quote.msg:
小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/

市面上的確好多paper話自己work但完全冇theory support 不過嗰啲9成垃圾

Sorry唔係好搵到mphil/phd level既鳩fit model paper 你搵到我都想見識下

...

俾一篇andrew ng出既垃圾paper大家欣賞下:

https://stanfordmlgroup.github.io/projects/chexnet/

呢篇paper用既database係呢個:

https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.02315

個annotation係用ML去gen出來, 再用gen出來既dataset去train一個classification model, 跟住話自己贏到醫生

https://lukeoakdenrayner.wordpress.com/2017/12/18/the-chestxray14-dataset-problems/comment-page-1/#comment-1430
呢個澳洲既放射科Phd醫生就睇完話有50% data係label錯既, 我自己check左一次呢個dataset都真係有咁多既錯label data

send過email過去問乜事, 跟住又係d中國人研究求其吹大個數字跟住俾NHS同Google拎左來做一個core dataset, 但條友已經走左, 對於佢呢個dataset annotation既研究不會有followup

然後全世界到今日都仲繼續用呢個錯label既dataset

玩Kaggle話接近現實果d, 一係住係象牙塔太耐, 一係未見過井外既世界

btw DS同software engineer係有分工, call library完全唔係問題, 成日覺得由要fundamental讀起乜都要尋根果d應該要學下level of abstraction, 唔好同我講你做NN連CUDNN, numpy, eigen, BLAS都唔call, 自己直接寫vector做LA運算

quote.msg:
Q: 係咪要睇曬所有書?
A: 唔係, 但應該睇曬/以前 course covered 過，如果發現有陌生 topic 就不妨開黎睇下，大學上堂其實好有限

Q: 數學好深喎，咁都夠膽講基礎?
A: Entschuldigung, 數學程度因人而異，建議慢慢黎 我只係敢講係 elementary + introductory，唔保證係 easy

Q: 但我出黎做嘢都唔洗咁多數學...
A: 的確係，正如你唔會信一個唔識 calculus 既 engineer 或者精算師，我都唔會信一位唔識 calculus 同 optimization 既 data scientist
calculus要識到咩程度先叫識

replies.msg: 唔同 area 需求都唔同，不過簡簡單單 chain rule 同 partial derivative 呢啲應該要知。唔係講緊要去到 analysis ，prove 曬啲 implicit function theorem approximation theorem，係講緊知道基本 differentiation 。不過總會有 on9 話知黎冇用，就算唔洗知 differentiation 都可以做 neural network，咁我講乜都曬氣架喎。如果連基本 differentiation 都唔識但搵到份 ML 工，只可以個人好彩囉

quote.msg:
小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/

replies.msg: 你唔係HR 黎既咩

再細心分析'連燈巴打'論點:

睇乜鬼書丫係連登攞存在感
睇書/介紹大家睇書 = 攞存在感，係虛榮又不實在既行為，引伸指睇書冇用
你當呢度真係大學個個都咁有motivation咩
開始假設遇到 bottleneck / 想學多啲既人唔存在，成個 population 無視咗，睇下 stat 差到咁既人竟然大談 machine learning
真係會睇d人唔洗等你講已經睇緊
等你講先睇d人又唔會睇得明又冇咁好耐性
係ML入面咁執著theory又係好天真
唔知佢睇邊啲對話睇到返黎執著 theory 係好天真，讀下 theory 就變成執著，呢類中文修辭手法同我環保 post 有得比
當實戰班友做到work但唔知點解既時候
一直唔講邊班友, 冇例子, 但好有趣佢會不斷問你拎例子
你就對住d simplified或者hypothetical既case打晒飛機
又開始假設我用緊 simplified / hypothetical case ，但又唔講咩 case，如果簡單 model 都做到 good performance ，搞咁複雜做咩姐? simplified case 完全唔係問題，hypothetical case 就唔知你 up 緊乜，feedforward neural network 本身係 pdp8 都係 hypothetical case 黎

而家又開始講 TA 啦，我唔敢擅自理解佢 TA 解乜。陣間佢轉一轉個 definition 就會由 Teaching assistant 變 Tarjan algorithms 又可能變 technical assistant 或者 talented analysis
咁你覺得最有可能係指緊咩？
你講就講清楚啲，陣間又話人地唔明你問題唔明你要嘅例子，然後又搬自己proposed 嗰套 "連燈仔極端論" ，我志在令你出醜嘅姐

認真唔係好知道上面咁多人喺度爲乜而拗，其實theory同application兩樣嘢都重要
不過學嘅過程同方法就好睇personal preference (嗰個都唔同，要自己探索)，你嘅end goal/objective係乜嘢(e.g. get a taste of convolutional neural networks with MNIST v.s. devise better approximations for second order gradient optimizers)同埋需要嘅level of abstraction (e.g. 寫custom TF operation要識寫cuDNN kernels for GPU optimization但係唔使一定要識GPU入面啲hardware optimizations).
仲有ML根本就係一個interdisciplinary field consisting of math(s) (e.g. mathematical properties of spatial-invariant NN), CS (e.g. NN in CV), stat (e.g. Bayesian deep learning), psychology (e.g. intrinsic motivation in RL), neuroscience (e.g. think computational modelling of the brain) etc., 唔同人就會對嗰field有唔同嘅見識，冇話邊個叻過邊個

That's it, let's move on. Stop digging the rabbit hole

replies.msg:
依家講緊apply啊傻仔 apply多數都係直接用㗎啦 你自己整到特別唔同architecture又有用咪上nature囉 無人話唔洗明 我咪話開始邊做邊學囉 只係apply你唔需要太明白model背後既嘢都可以開始先 專注揀feature training都要時間 你個陣睇書睇av又得

我意思係只要明白high level intuition其實已經可以開始 例如知道幾時overfit/underfit activation function點揀 幾時要揀regularization 幾時要多d training example 知道就得㗎啦唔需要下下走去proof㗎 人地話你知single hidden layer足夠大加上有non linear activation 就可以fit到任意function 唔通你又要去研究咩 前人做research就係要後人企係肩膀上 我只係作出取捨 有時間有需要你可以隨時睇多d 但太執著效率只會好慢 浪費前人well established咗既嘢

replies.msg:
懷疑你基本邏輯真係唔掂 浪費曬前人做嘅嘢。Regularization 又唔算 theory 嗰樣 Architecture 又唔算 theory 。分清楚 theory and theorem 先，theorem = sth can be proved，theory = a idea 。economics 都好多冇得 prove 嘅 theory 一份 paper就算冇 prove ，佢提出咗 formulation of 某類 neural network architecture ，或者另類 backpropagation 方法，或者一個 regularization scheme 或者ensemble learning method 都係 theory 黎

Parameter 只係 common sense Well, layman 會否一 call library 就知道 self organizing map 嘅 topology 呢？會否一 call library 就知道 variational autoencoder 呢？普通人都知嘅先算 common sense ，得你知人唔知嘅叫專業知識。打個比喻，你唔會將醫生斷證嘅邏輯當 common sense 掛？

你打咁大段嘢都係錯漏百出，想逗我笑定點...
錯埋 grammar 係 an idea

講咗咁耐嗰兩位巴打都係擅自將 theory
同 mathematical proof 劃上等號，各位學習緊 ML 嘅要注意呢個係錯誤理解

Economics, Social science, Science 都好多 theory 係冇 proof/formal justification ，佢地依然係 theory ，唔同 maths/stat 嘅 theorem。Theory 係關於一個 idea，就好似 feedforward neural network 咁，佢嘅 architecture ，propagation direction，hyperparameter 都係 theory 嘅一種，不過並非 theorem，同樣地 linear regression model 都係 theory 。theory 可以 involves theorem，neural network 都有 universal approximation theorem，但 theory 唔會因此變 theorem

支持

replies.msg:
平時就比較難答，工作做過 finance & retail 呢類 application ， 有 segmentation, classification 同 prediction，例如話一啲時間相關嘅 data 搵 pattern 同埋短暫 forecast，例如話老細想知呢堆人入面有咩消費特徵或者 churn prediction

不過講到 A.I. 其實有啲時候，某類 application ，都可以用傳統 statistics e.g. ANOVA, Survival analysis 。比 layman 更易 interpret

小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/

講咗咁耐嗰兩位巴打都係擅自將 theory
同 mathematical proof 劃上等號，各位學習緊 ML 嘅要注意呢個係錯誤理解

Economics, Social science, Science 都好多 theory 係冇 proof/formal justification ，佢地依然係 theory ，唔同 maths/stat 嘅 theorem。Theory 係關於一個 idea，就好似 feedforward neural network 咁，佢嘅 architecture ，propagation direction，hyperparameter 都係 theory 嘅一種，不過並非 theorem，同樣地 linear regression model 都係 theory 。theory 可以 involves theorem，neural network 都有 universal approximation theorem，但 theory 唔會因此變 theorem

小妹 sort 咗個 list for 免費同基礎既 ML/AI 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，先拋磚歡迎補充

Deep learning & AI:
Deep learning. Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/

Bayesian Reasoning and Machine Learning. David Barber
http://web4.cs.ucl.ac.uk/staff/D.Barber/pmwiki/pmwiki.php?n=Brml.HomePage

Graph Representation Learning Book. William L. Hamilton.
https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/

Introduction to Information Retrieval. Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan and Hinrich Schütze.
https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/

Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents. David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth.
https://artint.info/2e/html/ArtInt2e.html

Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
http://incompleteideas.net/book/the-book-2nd.html

Statistical learning theory:
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in R. Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie and Robert Tibshirani:
http://faculty.marshall.usc.edu/gareth-james/ISL/

The Elements of Statistical Learning: Data Mining, Inference, and Prediction. Trevor Hastie. Robert Tibshirani. Jerome Friedman
https://web.stanford.edu/~hastie/ElemStatLearn/

replies.msg:
1. 可能我講得太糢糊，Equity discounted cash flow 係冇 formal justification on estimating intrisinc value，只係 convention approach，一直以來都有好多 debates，詳細可以 google。MM theorem 只係基於 corp fin theory 嘅 premises & assumptions 以 mathematical ways 去證明，本身係 assume 緊 DCF 而非 justify DCF... Option pricing 都係用 DCF 而入面都夠多 theorem ，但呢堆 theorem 並無 justify DCF ，反而係建基於 Expected DCF 嘅 Assumption 去作出 evaluation

2. 我個人認同 nonlinear time series 係 theory 你搞錯咗啲嘢，我係話照返佢道理嘅話，nonlinear time series theory 好多嘢都冇 linear time series theory 咁齊 proof 齊asymptotic theory，有啲 even 係 work因為 practically work 。呢一點同 nn 有點似，重點在於指出，唔係所有嘢都要 proof 先係 theory

replies.msg:
p = E(mx)
p = E(m)E(x) + Cov(m, x) by the definition of Cov(.)
p = E(x) / R^f + Cov(m, x) as 1 = E(m R^f) <=> 1/R^f = E(m)

this is the DCF model..
for the formal justification of p = E(mx), see Chapter 3 and 4 of Cochrane, J. (2005). Asset Pricing (Revised Edition).

In fact, Cochrane's proof is not super rigorous, for the formal proof,
Hansen, L., & Richard, S. (1987). The Role of Conditioning Information in Deducing Testable Restrictions Implied by Dynamic Asset Pricing Models. Econometrica, 55(3), 587-613.

replies.msg:
今日終於睇左篇野
以我理解其實個idea幾簡單

Parameterization
首先一條protein當有L個residues (amino acids我估?)
未fold時就咁一條chain冇branch
所以fold完之後成個structure可以用第i同第i+1個residue之間既角度(torsion, 佢文中既phi_i同psi_i)黎parameterize
而知道consecutive既torsion之後可以計返每pair residue之間既距離(distance, 佢文中既d_{ij})
呢個係佢點parameterize個shape, 而個final output都係呢堆torsion

Idea
Step 1
佢主要learn既野有2個：
1. 直接predict torsion (phi, psi)
2. predict distance d_{ij} (note: distance其實係可以用torsion黎計, 但佢另外predict)
其中一個重點係，佢predict既唔係一個value, 佢係predict個conditional distribution p_t(phi, psi | S, MSA(S)) (torsion distribution)同p_d(d_{ij} | S, MSA(S)) (distance distribution)
(S係條protein sequence, MSA(S)係用條S係其他database度搵類似sequence，MSA stands for multiple sequence alignment，實際呢個"feature" intuitively有咩用我就唔知喇)
(明顯好處係佢可以用呢個distribution黎sample initial guess for later gradient descent based optimization)
即係個NN係估緊given條protein S同MSA result MSA(S)，distance同torsion既distribution (independently?)

Step 2
跟住佢用呢2個distribution乘埋黎做個optimization既objective function，intuitively呢個function係somewhat (but not exactly)代表緊given S同MSA(S), 每個torsion (i.e., shape)出現既probability，而maximize佢就係搵最有可能個torsion (shape)
Formally, 佢個objective function係p_d({d_{ij}} | S, MSA(S))*p_t(phi, psi | S, MSA(S)), variable係(phi, psi) vector
注意2樣野
i. 佢呢度個d_{ij}係用返(phi, psi)去計，而p_d唔係用p_t黎計，如果我冇理解錯的話
(我諗應該就係咁，因為唔係既話佢其實唔洗predict多次p_d，直接用p_t去計就計到p_d)
ii. 佢learn時係有可能係predict個*marginal* distribution而唔係個joint distribution，係既話佢呢度個p_d同p_t會係就咁將d marginal distribution乘埋晒
個情況就好似assume conditioning on S同MSA(S), d_{ij}同(phi_i, psi_i)係independent for all 1 <= i,j <= L

成個idea就係咁

Implementation
實際implement佢加左唔少野:
1. 另外learn一個protein-independent (但length dependent)既distance distribution q_d(d_{ij} | length)，最後真係用個distance distribution係p_d(d_{ij} | S, MSA(S))*q_d({d_{ij}} | length(S))
佢話目的係"correct overrepresentation" of the prior (p_d( . | S, MSA(S))?)
2. 成條sequence L個residue，個distance distribution p_d(d_{ij} | S, MSA(S))個p_d prediction NN唔係直接output L^2個distribution，佢係係L入面抽2組分別64個consecutive residue既segment A同B，之後個NN predict 64^2個distributions p_d(d_{ij} | S , MSA(S)) for all i in A and j in B，augmented by p_d(d_{ij} | S, MSA(S)) for all i,j in A同 for all i,j in B
(因為呢兩group既distance distribution好影響i in A同j in B呢堆既distribution)
咁做有2個好處: (i). 可以generate多好多samples, 一個protein sample佢可以generate多4千幾個samples; (ii). 減低memory需求 (佢話仲可以avoid overfitting)
3. NN係用deep ResConv2d
4. Optimization，呢度主要有3步：
(i) 佢係我上面講個2個distribution既product度再乘多左個distribution (佢話係關van der Waals時，唔知咩黎但我估係用黎避免就咁optimize時會出左d physically唔可能既shape)
(ii) 佢想用gradient descent去maximize個objective function，但因為本身佢NN出黎既係discretize左既probability distribution，唔differentiable，所以佢用cubic spline去fit p_d，用個叫von Mises distribution既class去fit p_t，而家個objective function (連埋4(i)個van der Waals term)係smooth，可以就咁gradient descent (btw佢take左negative log先，因為易d做gradient descent)
(iii) 用p_t去sample好多個initial guess，之後對上面(ii)個smoothed左個version做gradient descent，成個optimization係unconstrained
由呢堆initial guess搵到堆local minimum，係入面keep最好個90%加noise，再係p_t度sample多10%新既initial guess，重覆4(iii).直到converge

成個AlphaFold 1就係咁

睇佢AlphaFold 2個blog post，新版分別似乎用個"spatial graph"(which is "important for understanding the physical interactions within proteins, as well as their evolutionary history")去取代個torsion representation
跟住用個attention-based NN去predict呢個spatial graph structure

點解佢performance好有2個問題：
1. 係咪因為佢個objective function雖然唔係exact, 但for some reason佢有好多好既local minimum，所以佢做randomization既時候比較大機會中到個唔錯既?如果直接搵未smooth個global minimum (by exhaustive search)會唔會performance仲好？
2. 定其實係因為個distance同torsion distribution既prediction夠準？In particular個distance distribution既prediction有幾重要？例如如果我直接用個ground truth既去做會點？
3. 點解Conv2D適合用黎predict distance同torsion distribution?如果fully connected會點？
4 (你問既點解要咁parameterize). 用distance同torsion去parameterize係咪最好？似乎唔係，AlphaFold 2似乎係用其他野取代torsion，但仲有用distance

replies.msg:
tensorflow係虐新手既library來, 因為佢既設計logic唔係pythonic (at least 係session runtime下係咁樣, eager mode/ keras會正常D)

tensorflow設計logic係將每個math operation設計成一個節點(node), 呢d node就係好似城市水管網既一個水流控制咁, 會將流入既水(tensor)進行數學運算

利用呢d ops去堆壘成一個巨型既network,又由於大部份ops都係differentiable,咁就可以構成一張好大既compute graph, 相對應於一個大型既NN, 由於node differentiable既特性就可以用返back propagation既chain rule特性做optimization

到直正運算時, 你會有一個類似城市既水管系統, 但係每個節點既水量調節一開始係隨機設定, 係排水口你會有個對應入水量既desire排水量, 你目標係就要自動代去修正節點數去得出排水口正確量

測量排水口同expected量既function就係所謂既optimization/loss function, 而tune水閘既方式就係叫optimizer

呢套系統強大在於係核心用C++寫成oop既ops, 可以輕易deploy於分佈運算系統入面, 同制作相應既other language interface,例如python, javascript, 你完成左以上既水管鋪網同參數修正就可以成張圖壓縮同轉移去其他平台使用

如果想學基本野就睇呢個:http://web.stanford.edu/class/cs20si/
stanford出既tensorflow課程, 係行session runtime, 相對係底層既教學

至於實戰...睇tensorflow官網教學會好少少, 但係tensorflow同pytorch最大分別係api成日都改, 唔同version既code唔係100% compatible, 仲有唔同人既寫法都好大出入, 對新手學習係好大既阻礙

quote.msg:
tensorflow係虐新手既library來, 因為佢既設計logic唔係pythonic (at least 係session runtime下係咁樣, eager mode/ keras會正常D)

tensorflow設計logic係將每個math operation設計成一個節點(node), 呢d node就係好似城市水管網既一個水流控制咁, 會將流入既水(tensor)進行數學運算

利用呢d ops去堆壘成一個巨型既network,又由於大部份ops都係differentiable,咁就可以構成一張好大既compute graph, 相對應於一個大型既NN, 由於node differentiable既特性就可以用返back propagation既chain rule特性做optimization

到直正運算時, 你會有一個類似城市既水管系統, 但係每個節點既水量調節一開始係隨機設定, 係排水口你會有個對應入水量既desire排水量, 你目標係就要自動代去修正節點數去得出排水口正確量

測量排水口同expected量既function就係所謂既optimization/loss function, 而tune水閘既方式就係叫optimizer

呢套系統強大在於係核心用C++寫成oop既ops, 可以輕易deploy於分佈運算系統入面, 同制作相應既other language interface,例如python, javascript, 你完成左以上既水管鋪網同參數修正就可以成張圖壓縮同轉移去其他平台使用

如果想學基本野就睇呢個:http://web.stanford.edu/class/cs20si/
stanford出既tensorflow課程, 係行session runtime, 相對係底層既教學

至於實戰...睇tensorflow官網教學會好少少, 但係tensorflow同pytorch最大分別係api成日都改, 唔同version既code唔係100% compatible, 仲有唔同人既寫法都好大出入, 對新手學習係好大既阻礙

1. Discounted cash flow 點解冇formal justification?
MM theorem 都係depends on discounted cash flow

2. nonlinear time series 點解唔係theory??
see below for asymptotic theory of threshold AR model
Hansen, B.E. (2000), Sample Splitting and Threshold Estimation. Econometrica, 68: 575-603.

am i too serious?

1. 可能我講得太糢糊，Equity discounted cash flow 係冇 formal justification on estimating intrisinc value，只係 convention approach，一直以來都有好多 debates，詳細可以 google。MM theorem 只係基於 corp fin theory 嘅 premises & assumptions 以 mathematical ways 去證明，本身係 assume 緊 DCF 而非 justify DCF... Option pricing 都係用 DCF 而入面都夠多 theorem ，但呢堆 theorem 並無 justify DCF ，反而係建基於 Expected DCF 嘅 Assumption 去作出 evaluation

2. 我個人認同 nonlinear time series 係 theory 你搞錯咗啲嘢，我係話照返佢道理嘅話，nonlinear time series theory 好多嘢都冇 linear time series theory 咁齊 proof 齊asymptotic theory，有啲 even 係 work因為 practically work 。呢一點同 nn 有點似，重點在於指出，唔係所有嘢都要 proof 先係 theory

p = E(mx)
p = E(m)E(x) + Cov(m, x) by the definition of Cov(.)
p = E(x) / R^f + Cov(m, x) as 1 = E(m R^f) <=> 1/R^f = E(m)

this is the DCF model..
for the formal justification of p = E(mx), see Chapter 3 and 4 of Cochrane, J. (2005). Asset Pricing (Revised Edition).

In fact, Cochrane's proof is not super rigorous, for the formal proof,
Hansen, L., & Richard, S. (1987). The Role of Conditioning Information in Deducing Testable Restrictions Implied by Dynamic Asset Pricing Models. Econometrica, 55(3), 587-613.

小弟 sort 咗個 list for 基礎既 ML/AI 用到既數學 既書。當然 advanced 既 topic 係要睇 research paper，因為書通常都 update 唔切，歡迎補充


Q: 係咪要睇曬所有書?
A: 唔係, 但應該睇曬/以前 course covered 過，如果發現有陌生 topic 就不妨開黎睇下，大學上堂其實好有限

Q: 數學好深喎，咁都夠膽講基礎?
A: Entschuldigung, 數學程度因人而異，建議慢慢黎 我只係敢講係 elementary + introductory，唔保證係 easy，但係我想講如果你唔打好個基礎，到你睇optimization時連咩係objective function都唔知！

Q: 但我出黎做嘢都唔洗咁多數學...
A: 的確係，正如你唔會信一個唔識 calculus 既 engineer 或者精算師，我都唔會信一位唔識 mathematical logic, analysis, probability theory, algorithm 同 combinatorics 既data scientist

ching你問錯人了，其實樓主係唔識扮識的，不論technical定theoretical skill都好屎
所謂maximum likelihood, 個idea係去maximum個"likelihood function"
好多情況之下你見到既野都depends on一個unknown parameter
例如(樓主)鳩答答到一舊舊既次數(observable outcome)可以係depends on佢既IQ (unknown parameter)
假設我地有一個合理既probability distribution去model IQ同鳩答次數既關係
e.g., p(x | IQ = y) = 假設IQ只有y既情況下，樓主鳩答x次既機會
呢個probability可以interpret做，當IQ只有y既時候，有幾"likely"樓主會鳩答x次
叫p(x | y)做likelihood function
而家當你只觀察到x但想估IQ y係幾多時
其中一個intuitively make sense既做法係去搵IQ y that maximize個likelihood function
相等於你試唔同既IQ數值y, 放落個function p(x | y)度check (note: 你知已知鳩答次數x係幾多)，睇下邊個y會俾到p(x | y)最高既數值，跟住就用呢個y黎做樓主IQ既預測

呢個只係一個intuitively make sense既estimation方法
咁做有咩好處係要justify的
turns out呢種estimation方法有某d theoretical guarantee, 例如(under some conditions) sample數夠多你estimate出黎個parameter真係會趨向true parameter value

除maximum likelihood另外有個approach叫maximum a posteriori (MAP)，idea係睇p(y | x) instead of p(x | y)
p(x |y) 係likelihood function, p(y| x)係代表緊，假設已知鳩答次數係x次既情況下，樓主IQ既distribution係點
MAP idea就係去搵y that maximum p(y| x)
但一般情況y -> x個關係會比較易容直接model
(例如樓主IQ越低，佢鳩答次數會越多，對應y越細, x越大)
而y -> x唔容易直接model, 所以都係用Bayes rule搵：p(y |x) = p(x|y)p(y)/p(x)
不過呢度你要model埋parameter y個distribution p(y) (叫priori distribution)
事實上你maximize p(y|x)同maximize likelihood係差唔多，個分別只係你多左個額外既"regularization" term p(y)
原來除咗扮識 quantum 仲會扮識 bio

p = E(mx)
p = E(m)E(x) + Cov(m, x) by the definition of Cov(.)
p = E(x) / R^f + Cov(m, x) as 1 = E(m R^f) <=> 1/R^f = E(m)

this is the DCF model..
for the formal justification of p = E(mx), see Chapter 3 and 4 of Cochrane, J. (2005). Asset Pricing (Revised Edition).

In fact, Cochrane's proof is not super rigorous, for the formal proof,
Hansen, L., & Richard, S. (1987). The Role of Conditioning Information in Deducing Testable Restrictions Implied by Dynamic Asset Pricing Models. Econometrica, 55(3), 587-613.

replies.msg:
1. You claim that FCFE is the payoff to investors. Does an investor really receive FCFE per share? It depends on the dividend policy and share buyback policy. I do agree FCFE can be an estimate for payoff as a practice. However, the discounted sum of FCFE does not really estimate intrinsic value. You agree on it as you avoid to mention it again.

2. What I stated at the beginning (after the correction), is the current DCF for equity is just a convention instead of something based on mathematical proof or formal justification. Have you really looked into any DCF models for equity in reality?

3. The reference book, recommended by you, mentions that LOOP and p = E[Mx] in an 'if and only if' sense. Under MM theorem, it is based on an efficient market where LOOP holds. Hence, p = E[Mx] holds as well. (In the original text, Modigliani and Miller used the word 'perfect market'. Hope you read it). Uh huh? What's wrong with this statement? Moreover, you are trying to reject my modified argument by MM theorem. Let me quote it here: "Discounted cash flow 點解冇formal justification? MM theorem 都係depends on discounted cash flow". You don't need to feel ashamed for changing or modifying your arguments.

4. I didn't claim my theoretical knowledge is always flawless. Thanks for your recommendation, and as a gift in return, I would like to recommend you an online course on logic as well:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/logic-introduction

5. I understand what you're saying. And that's why I am focusing on equity DCF instead of option DCF. I use it as an example to illustrate that you cannot prove DCF theory in a rigorous sense from the theorems option pricing. Please don't take it twisted.

6. You cannot reject my point by neglecting FCFF as well. However, I am not going to challenge you by asking a question with an obvious answer. I don't want to ruin this post by talking too much about corporate finance.

GMM and maximum likelihood are two different things.

Gaussian Mixture Model 唔係用 EM algorithms 搵返唔同 basis 嘅 mean 同 variance 咩懷疑你誤會咗我講 Generalized Method of Moment. MoM 同 MLE 當然係唔野
GMM normally refers to Generalized Method of Moment.

replies.msg: I don't know how you define 'normally' and link generalized method of moment to maximum likelihood and EM algorithm in a magical way. It's good that you point out generalized method of moment to avoid ambiguity.

"Now, assume that the investor can freely buy or sell as much of the
payoff xt+1 as he wishes, at a price pt. " (Asset Pricing)

而 intrinsic value 其中採用 Aswarth Damodaran 嘅 definition:
"On the first question, here is my definition of intrinsic value. It is the value that you would attach to an asset, based upon its fundamentals: cash flows, expected growth and risk. "

再者 discounted cash flow 係 p = E(mx)
x 係 payoff 但 equity discounted cash flow 常用嘅 free cash flow to equity/free cash flow 都唔係 investors payoff，即使 discounted dividend model 都只係 investor partial payoff 所以 point 1 is valid

此外，Option pricing 入面確實係建基於 expected discounted cash flow 嘅 Assumption 去做 valuation ，但本身 theorem 並無去 justify DCF 本身。所以要收返 point 2 invalid 嘅說法

再者 MM theorem proof based on no arbitrage。

"The first theorem states that there is a discount factor that prices all the payoffs by p = E(mx) if and only if this law of one price holds." (Asset Pricing)

Wiki 未必good ref 但足夠矣

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Modigliani%E2%80%93Miller_theorem

感覺你係讀好多書嘅人黎，但係有啲未消化到

Free cash flow to equity holder is a payoff to the investor. Do you really understand what is dcf model? My point is that every asset pricing model can be mapped to the framework of p = E(Mx) while this can be justified by some simple assumptions. What you stated above is one of the theorem related to p=E(Mx). My feeling is that your theoretical knowledge is not concrete, recommend reading more Cochran’s Asset Pricing is a good start

1. You claim that FCFE is the payoff to investors. Does an investor really receive FCFE per share? It depends on the dividend policy and share buyback policy. I do agree FCFE can be an estimate for payoff as a practice. However, the discounted sum of FCFE does not really estimate intrinsic value. You agree on it as you avoid to mention it again.

2. What I stated at the beginning (after the correction), is the current DCF for equity is just a convention instead of something based on mathematical proof or formal justification. Have you really looked into any DCF models for equity in reality?

3. The reference book, recommended by you, mentions that LOOP and p = E[Mx] in an 'if and only if' sense. Under MM theorem, it is based on an efficient market where LOOP holds. Hence, p = E[Mx] holds as well. (In the original text, Modigliani and Miller used the word 'perfect market'. Hope you read it). Uh huh? What's wrong with this statement? Moreover, you are trying to reject my modified argument by MM theorem. Let me quote it here: "Discounted cash flow 點解冇formal justification? MM theorem 都係depends on discounted cash flow". You don't need to feel ashamed for changing or modifying your arguments.

4. I didn't claim my theoretical knowledge is always flawless. Thanks for your recommendation, and as a gift in return, I would like to recommend you an online course on logic as well:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/logic-introduction

5. I understand what you're saying. And that's why I am focusing on equity DCF instead of option DCF. I use it as an example to illustrate that you cannot prove DCF theory in a rigorous sense from the theorems option pricing. Please don't take it twisted.

6. You cannot reject my point by neglecting FCFF as well. However, I am not going to challenge you by asking a question with an obvious answer. I don't want to ruin this post by talking too much about corporate finance.

replies.msg:
I have already shown that dcf model is only a special case of p = E(Mx). Fcfe is the potential payoff to the shareholder, of course may not equal to dividend. So, Dcf model has formal justification as long as p = E(Mx) is formally justified. There is nothing related to the real world application of Dcf model as your statement is about formal justification.

For the MM theorem, the discount rate is different when the firm is leveraged. However, the firm value is the same (without tax). The firm value is calculated by DCF liked model. I am not talking about the assumptions (perfect market, etc.) you mentioned.

quote.msg: Free cash flow to equity holder is a payoff to the investor. Do you really understand what is dcf model? My point is that every asset pricing model can be mapped to the framework of p = E(Mx) while this can be justified by some simple assumptions. What you stated above is one of the theorem related to p=E(Mx). My feeling is that your theoretical knowledge is not concrete, recommend reading more Cochran’s Asset Pricing is a good start

1. You claim that FCFE is the payoff to investors. Does an investor really receive FCFE per share? It depends on the dividend policy and share buyback policy. I do agree FCFE can be an estimate for payoff as a practice. However, the discounted sum of FCFE does not really estimate intrinsic value. You agree on it as you avoid to mention it again.

2. What I stated at the beginning (after the correction), is the current DCF for equity is just a convention instead of something based on mathematical proof or formal justification. Have you really looked into any DCF models for equity in reality?

3. The reference book, recommended by you, mentions that LOOP and p = E[Mx] in an 'if and only if' sense. Under MM theorem, it is based on an efficient market where LOOP holds. Hence, p = E[Mx] holds as well. (In the original text, Modigliani and Miller used the word 'perfect market'. Hope you read it). Uh huh? What's wrong with this statement? Moreover, you are trying to reject my modified argument by MM theorem. Let me quote it here: "Discounted cash flow 點解冇formal justification? MM theorem 都係depends on discounted cash flow". You don't need to feel ashamed for changing or modifying your arguments.

4. I didn't claim my theoretical knowledge is always flawless. Thanks for your recommendation, and as a gift in return, I would like to recommend you an online course on logic as well:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/logic-introduction

5. I understand what you're saying. And that's why I am focusing on equity DCF instead of option DCF. I use it as an example to illustrate that you cannot prove DCF theory in a rigorous sense from the theorems option pricing. Please don't take it twisted.

6. You cannot reject my point by neglecting FCFF as well. However, I am not going to challenge you by asking a question with an obvious answer. I don't want to ruin this post by talking too much about corporate finance.

I have already shown that dcf model is only a special case of p = E(Mx). Fcfe is the potential payoff to the shareholder, of course may not equal to dividend. So, Dcf model has formal justification as long as p = E(Mx) is formally justified. There is nothing related to the real world application of Dcf model as your statement is about formal justification.

For the MM theorem, the discount rate is different when the firm is leveraged. However, the firm value is the same (without tax). The firm value is calculated by DCF liked model. I am not talking about the assumptions (perfect market, etc.) you mentioned.

Plz reread my post before replying. It seems that you dun really get what I said

the p in p = E(mx) is the "true" price at time t,
the actual price can deviate from this price if the market is not efficient (enough). So, I am talking about "intrinsic" value instead of actual price.

Terminal value and "grow forever" is a kind of assumption. It is tangential here as discussion focuses on "formal justification" of DCF model.

referring to your previous reply, "you cannot prove DCF theory in a rigorous sense from the theorems option pricing"
I have never said that.
In fact, according to my understanding, option pricing model (like BS formula) can be derived from simple valuation formula (DCF liked) formula, plz read:
Rubinstein, M. (1976). The Valuation of Uncertain Income Streams and the Pricing of Options. The Bell Journal of Economics, 7(2), 407-425.

pinned.msg:
入門嘅ML主要都係要識 Gaussian distribution/conditional probability/Bayes theorem 同埋 differentiation/integration/少少partial differentiation (簡單嗰啲夠皮，唔使integration by part)。不過最緊要最緊要最緊要都係matrix algebra, 啲eigenvalue 同 eigenvector 成日周不時都會走嚟搵你㗎。Programming 嘅話就一定係Python，因爲Python有好多library畀你玩。

個人覺得冇話邊本書對初學者嚟講係最好，因爲我都係一路睇網上面啲blog/tutorial (medium.com 好多都有用圖嚟解釋啲algorithm，比起textbook冇咁悶) 同埋example code玩玩下學返嚟。learn by doing始終都係會有大啲成功感，起碼唔會俾啲proof of correctness同啲algorithm嘅implementation detail嚇親搞到冇嗮癮先。
ML有個好處就在於你可以將啲ML algorithm攞嚟plug + play，當lego嚟砌，所以有時就真係未必要食嗮全部theory先可以砌model，當然做研究另計啦。

btw colab.research.google.com 是個好東西，可以喺browser度寫Python code同有限度噉免費使用GPU。唔得嘅話就可以用Jupyter notebook，依啲嘢好多時data science係都會用嘅
